metaTitle: Changelog — Tribulation | Difficulty Overhaul for Minecraft
metaDescription: Release history for Tribulation: every released version with its changes, linked to the canonical GitHub Releases page.
ogDescription: Release history for Tribulation with per-version changes.
title: Changelog
lede: Every released version. Jars and full notes live on GitHub Releases — the canonical download source.

html: The first patch release: the difficulty level moves out of chat and onto your screen.

items: Level HUD overlay — a configurable on-screen badge showing your Tribulation level, color-coded by tier, with anchor and pixel-offset settings (#5)
Release tags switched to the short vX.Y.Z format (#3)
Version bumped to 0.1.1 (#6)

blocks.html: Download: tribulation-0.1.1.jar · full diff

html: The initial public release — the full difficulty overhaul: three-axis mob scaling (time, distance, height) with per-mob rates and caps for 21 vanilla hostile mobs, tier-gated abilities at the five level thresholds, special zombie variants, boss scaling, bonus XP, and the opt-in death penalty systems (Death Relief, Shatter Shards, Hardcore Hearts, Soul Inventory).

items: Dedicated testing pipeline — unit tests and Fabric gametests run on every PR (#1)
Claude GitHub Actions integration for automated code review (#2)

html: Tribulation follows semantic versioning. The public API surface (com.rfizzle.tribulation.api) is stable across patch and minor releases; a breaking API change requires a major version bump and a changelog entry naming the broken signature.
